examples: This radical attacks the carbonyl group to an intermediate bicyclic ketyl.
The 4-methoxy-phenylhydroxy-acetic acid was synthesized by the carbonyl addition reaction of 4-methoxybenzaldehyde.
The temperature of carbonyl synthesis reaction is studied.
Although carbonyl formation is a major oxidative modification induced by peroxynitrite, nitration of tyrosine residues is an indicator of peroxynitrite involvement.
Peroxynitrite is a source of hydroxyl radical-like reactivity, and it directly oxidizes proteins and other macromolecules with resultant carbonyl formation from side-chain and peptide-bond cleavage.
